I replaced a 5-person dev team with myself + Claude Code and Codex.

The product shipped in two weeks. Not a prototype — a production platform live with real users right now.

Thread on what actually happened
What is the most surprising thing about building real products with AI coding agents? It's not how astonishingly fast and capable they are. It's...

The point is that the skills that matter most today in AI-native development are the same ones that matter in traditional product development. Clear communication. Good product judgment. Systems thinking. Great documentation.

This is actually good news if you're a product leader, designer, or engineering manager. The tools are different but the craft is the same. How the work gets done is *totally* different, but your experience isn't obsolete... it's probably more valuable than ever.

The question companies should be asking isn't "how do we make our eng teams 20% more productive with AI?"

It's "what happens when a single senior product leader who deeply understands a domain can build the entire product themselves?"

If you're a product leader, a technical founder, a CTO — try building something end-to-end with these tools.

Not as a side experiment. As the actual thing. You might surprise yourself.

Happy to share more about the technical setup and what's working (and what isn't).

Some context: I've been a product/tech leader for 20 years — Apple, Disney+, Airbnb, founded an AI startup (acq'd).

I know what it takes to ship with a real team. The standups, sprint planning, PR reviews, alignment meetings, the back-and-forth between product, design, and eng.

Round 3: No search 

We stripped web access. "What do you actually believe?"

Market crashed 65% → 50%.

•⁠  ⁠Claude: traded anyway — 7/8 bets on Patriots
•⁠  ⁠GPT-5.2: 19/20 held
•⁠  ⁠Gemini: 20/20 held
•⁠  ⁠Grok: only contrarians acted

Round 4: Full research autonomy 

Each agent wrote its own queries, read full articles, found gaps, did follow-up research, then decided.

64 of 80 refused to trade. They concluded "market is fair."

Market drifted to 47% — Patriots slightly favored.

Is GPT 5.4 really good?

I used codex-5.4-extra-high to fine-tune qwen-3.5-4b. (SFT)

(Exhausted all my pro plan weekly credits in 24 hours.)

And also used opus-4.6 to fine-tune qwen-3.5-9b

Codex is fast but dataset quality is crap.

Opus is slow but data quality is great.

This took off.

I'm running 4-bit quantized variant of Qwen 3.5 4B on my macbook pro m3 with 24 GB unified memory. 

It is giving me 40 tokens/sec of complex tasks and 48 tokens/sec on linear tasks. Feels instant.
